what_it_does: Declares a selectable generation policy named prose_default that configures a single-rung repetition-penalty ladder, disables diff-against-source and JSON-schema constraints, and sets a low temperature for free-prose/reduce/consult output.
observations: The module exports a single top-level GENERATION_POLICY dictionary with no executable code.
The policy id is prose_default and the docstring states it must equal the file stem.
The rep_penalty_ladder is deliberately a single rung [1.1], not an escalating ladder.
Both diff_against_source and json_schema are explicitly set to False.
The docstring frames the policy as a second regime to exercise per-content variation, not a global constant.
standouts: Single-rung [1.1] rep_penalty_ladder chosen because free prose is seen as less loop-prone.
Explicitly disables diff-against_source and json_schema on the grounds that prose is not enumerative.
The id/file-stem equivalence requirement is called out in the docstring.
conventions: Follows the convention of exporting a top-level GENERATION_POLICY data structure.
Uses a module docstring to explain registry relationship and design rationale.
Keeps the policy id consistent with the file stem.
Breaks any implicit convention that rep_penalty_ladder must be multi-rung, by design.
concerns: No in-file enforcement that id matches the file stem.
Numeric values (0.3, 1.1) are hardcoded without derivation or bounds documentation.
The policy dictionary lacks a version or schema-version field.
notes: This file is the free-prose generation-policy seed: a minimal, low-temperature, single-rung configuration meant for reduce/consult summaries.
questions: How is this policy selected over other regimes at runtime?
What is the exact contract and valid range for rep_penalty_ladder entries?
Are there other regimes that similarly disable both diff_against_source and json_schema?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — a free-prose, non-enumerative generation regime with a single-rung ladder and reduced constraints — matches the exported GENERATION_POLICY exactly.
intention_signals: It is trying to show that the generation-policy registry supports lightweight, surface-specific configurations rather than one global constant, and that prose regimes can safely omit grammar/diff guards.
novelty: Distinct in explicitly disabling both diff-against-source and json_schema and collapsing the repetition-penalty ladder to a single fixed rung, whereas grammar-constrained regimes would presumably use multi-rung ladders and schema/diff guards.
contribution: Provides a concrete, minimal prose-oriented policy record that a fused system would keep as one selectable regime for free-text reduce/consult content, demonstrating how policy parameters vary by surface type.
summary_for_embedding: A generation policy seed module that defines the prose_default regime with a single 1.1 repetition-penalty rung, no diff-against-source, no JSON schema, and temperature 0.3 for free-prose output.
intention_for_embedding: To register a low-constraint, low-loop-risk generation policy for free-prose reduce/consult summaries that varies policy flags from grammar-constrained regimes.
